mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate earnings downgrades, driving negative equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high inflation and collapsing housing starts indicate demand destruction outpacing supply adjustments, creating stagflationary conditions that compress equity multiples and drive negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when both high inflation and housing declines occur simultaneously, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average signals manufacturing sector contraction, which historically precedes broader economic slowdowns and negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not negatively correlated with INDPRO breaches of its moving average, or the signal generates false breakdowns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent annualized rate sustains corporate earnings momentum and supports risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarters where GDP growth exceeds 2.5 percent, or the signal shows no directional consistency over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly consumer price inflation forces market participants to price in aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, causing immediate equity risk-off reactions and negative forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat following monthly inflation accelerations above 0.4 percent, or the signal fails to predict negative returns in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a stable unemployment rate indicates a resilient labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative when both payrolls growth and unemployment stability are strong, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid two-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand that precedes corporate earnings revisions downward, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when retail sales contract rapidly, or the signal generates more false breakdowns than true signals over a 3-year evaluation period.
